tasks.description: Implement skeleton-config bootstrap per DEC-0001 / FGAP-001. In packages/pi-context/src/context.ts add writeSkeletonConfig (never-clobber, mints substrate_id, writes via writeConfigForDir) + isSkeletonConfig predicate, and change adoptConception's guard to if(existing && !isSkeletonConfig(existing)). In src/index.ts have initProject (248-298) and switchAndCreate (548-575) call writeSkeletonConfig after the dir-scaffold loop and update their completion/notify messages. In src/context-sdk.ts add the 'skeleton' state to the BootstrapStatus union and to deriveBootstrapState's ordered checks. In src/ops-registry.ts update the context-bootstrap-state (and context-init / context-accept-all as needed) description strings. Rework accept-all.test.ts idempotent + 'no defaults' tests, keep the no-config test in context-sdk.test.ts and add a skeleton-state test, add unit tests for isSkeletonConfig + init/switch-c skeleton writes + first amend-config add on a skeleton. Full completion sequence: build, check, test, runtime demo against a throwaway substrate, adversarial probe, regen skills. Implemented by a foreground coding subagent from the approved plan; orchestrator runs all npm and is sole git writer; per-step commit.
tasks.notes: Implemented at ab05a0e (pi-context skeleton-config bootstrap). Build/check/test green; runtime-demoed via the cli (custom path without accept-all; init->accept-all id-preservation + idempotence); a fresh adversarial re-audit found the isSkeletonConfig registry-class fix complete with no new regression.

tasks.description: Implement switch identity reconciliation per DEC-0003 / FGAP-003: switchToExisting and switchAndCreate register config.substrate_id -> active dir via registerSubstrate when the id is absent from the project-root registry; a present-but-different-dir entry continues to raise substrate_id_registry_mismatch. Tests: switch to an unregistered-but-present-config substrate -> registered + context-validate clean; mismatch case preserved. Full completion sequence (build/check/test/runtime-demo/adversarial/skills). Via coding subagent; orchestrator runs all npm + is sole git writer; per-step commit.
tasks.notes: Implemented at 17d9a3b. reconcileActiveSubstrateRegistration wired into all three switch engine functions (switchToExisting, switchAndCreate, switchToPrevious). Build/check/test green; cli runtime demo across all three paths (register-if-absent, mismatch-preserved, switch- heals pruned-previous); a fresh adversarial re-audit (after the probe found switchToPrevious unwired) confirmed all three paths consistent + no-previous error path intact + no new defect. One of the two items in the 0.29.0 cut.

tasks.description: Implement the substrate clone/import arc per DEC-0002 / FGAP-002 / FEAT-001: cloneSubstrate library fn (copy config + all blocks + relations.json + objects/ into the destination dir; mint a fresh substrate_id; record a substrate_derived_from_substrate edge; write a provenance-bearing registry entry) + Pi tool + CLI + orchestrator script as one dual-surface unit; register the substrate_derived_from_substrate relation_type in config; extend the registry schema/type with origin/provenance fields; refine the SoT-drift invariant (context-sdk.ts:1685+) to distinguish native-lost-registry / foreign-import / genuine drift. Full completion sequence. Via coding subagent; orchestrator runs all npm + is sole git writer; per-step commit.

Implement the git merge driver + post-merge integrity pass per DEC-0004 / FGAP-004 / FEAT-002: .gitattributes registration (substrate files -> merge=pi-context) + a [merge "pi-context"] driver entry; the driver entrypoint consuming git's %O/%A/%B/%P and writing a data-model-valid 3-way merge (items matched by id/oid, field-kind dispatch incl. the convergent sequence field via TASK-005, edge-set merge on relations.json, config/schema merge); a post-merge hook / merge-finalize command doing cross-block referential integrity + full invariant + lens revalidation and emitting a typed conflict set. Driver + per-file merge logic live in block-api; the post-merge pass is a git hook / CLI command. Merge-base, branches, refs, cherry-pick are git's, not reimplemented. Full completion sequence. Via coding subagent; orchestrator runs all npm + is sole git writer; per-step commit.

Implement the convergent ordered-sequence field-kind per DEC-0005 / FGAP-005 / FEAT-003: non-interleaving Fugue-class sequence CRDT over item-refs (dense immutable per-element position identifiers, insert mints one id + mutates no neighbor); Kleppmann move-CRDT position register resolved by hybrid logical clock (stable element identity; concurrent same-element moves converge); HLC for replica-independent same-gap ordering + move resolution; sort-on-read projection to a plain ordered ref-array + translation of array-position hand-edits to CRDT ops, through block-api; declared sequence-field kind recognized by schema; CRDT state stored in the owning <block>.json. Plugs into the substrate merge driver's field-merge dispatch. Full completion sequence. Via coding subagent; orchestrator runs all npm + is sole git writer; per-step commit. Depends on the substrate merge driver (TASK-004).

Implement DispatchContext threading through the op-execution contract per DEC-0006 / FGAP-008: add a ctx/writer channel to OpDefinition.run + registerAll (thread the pi ExtensionContext verified identity) + the pi-context-cli dispatch (build DispatchContext from --writer); forward ctx in all 10 ctx-dropping op handlers (append/update/remove-block-item, the nested trio, write-block, append-relation, amend-config, write-schema) and the pi-workflows step-block.ts writers; fix context-archive's declared-but-ignored writer. Full completion sequence. Via coding subagent; orchestrator runs all npm + is sole git writer; per-step commit.

Optional ctx?:DispatchContext on OpDefinition.run; cli builds it from --writer/resolveIdentity, in-pi registerAll from auth-gate params.writer else ctx.model agent; 10 write handlers forward it; pi-workflows step-block + artifact write stamp workflow-kind; promote-item/write-schema-migration converged; context-archive writer field removed; malformed --writer validated. build/check/test green; cli runtime demo (unstamped item onto attested-required schema now succeeds + stamps); adversarial probe confirmed all 10 ops stamp at correct arg position, dryRun preserved, no regression; two non-functional findings fixed + re-verified. cli arc alpha; beta (FGAP-009) + gamma (FGAP-010) remain.

tasks.description: FGAP-013 fix — CLI --json structured output (no double-encode). Introduce OpResult = string | {json:unknown} and widen OpDefinition.run. Split serializeForRead (read-element.ts) into structureForRead (data + cap/pagination metadata, un-stringified) + renderReadText (content + footer). 19 JSON.stringify ops return {json:result}; 16 serializeForRead ops return {json: structureForRead(...)}; 14 prose ops unchanged. Shared renderOpResultText used by registerAll (Pi-tool text byte-unchanged) and the CLI default branch; CLI --json emits typeof r===string ? r : r.json. Add a cli.test contract test locking --json output as a JSON value. Per the approved plan; one subsystem (op-output contract). Full completion sequence; coding subagent; orchestrator runs all npm + sole git writer.
acceptance_criteria: To be determined exactly from live codebase.

tasks.description: FGAP-015 fix — enforce the read cap at the output boundary (per DEC-0009). Three coordinated parts: (1) runtime boundary cap boundOpResult applied at the 3 emit sites (cli.ts --json :492, cli.ts text :497, registerAll Pi-tool :1927) — {read} passes through, {json}/prose serialized + byte-checked via truncateHead, over-cap fail-closes (structured {data:null,truncated:true,totalBytes,note} for --json; REFUSAL prose for text/Pi-tool); (2) route resolve-item-by-id + promote-item through structureForRead/{read} for graceful degradation; (3) parity-check gate rejecting an op that directly returns {json} of a content-reading lib call (readBlock/readBlockItem/resolveItemById/filterBlockItems/joinBlocks/resolveItemsByIds/buildIdIndex/resolveRef). Add Pi-tool over-cap test (uncovered surface). Lands with held TASK-012 as the complete --json contract; closes FGAP-013 + FGAP-015. Full completion sequence; coding subagent; orchestrator runs all npm + sole git writer + re-audit.
acceptance_criteria: To be determined exactly from live codebase.

tasks.description: FGAP-014 fix — migrate completeTask to the verification_verifies_item edge (per FGAP-014). completeTask (context-sdk.ts) currently reads verification.target/target_type, removed by c08f93b for the edge and never migrated, so it is unusable on any catalog substrate. Rewrite as a pure edge-gate: find verification (exists), assert status===passed, find task (exists, not completed/cancelled), ASSERT a verification_verifies_item edge (parent=verificationId, child=taskId) via findReferencesInRepo/walkAncestorsByLens (throw a clear edge-absent error if missing), set task status:completed via updateItemInBlock (DROP the verification field embed — the edge is the linkage), thread ctx for attestation. complete-task op: add ctx to run() + thread. Rewrite the 12-test suite to the edge-based flow (schema-valid verifications, no smuggled target/target_type; file the edge via appendRelationByRef; wrong-task -> no-edge; drop wrong-type; add a no-edge-bypass case). Full completion sequence; coding subagent; orchestrator runs all npm + sole git writer + re-audit. Closes FGAP-014.
acceptance_criteria: To be determined exactly from live codebase.

tasks.description: CLI pre-call input layer: normalize flag forms, derive arrayKey from config, and accept the scripts' input shorthands so the CLI reaches script-parity on INPUT without changing in-pi ops. All edits live in cli.ts's pre-call path (parseOpArgs ~:184-233 + the required-filter exemption ~:293-303). Closes FGAP-019 (arrayKey required-but-derivable across 7 block-mutation ops — derive from config.block_kinds[].array_key, demote --arrayKey to optional override by extending the required-filter exemption from {writer} to {writer,arrayKey} + an injectArrayKey helper mirroring injectWriter), FGAP-025 (--writer kind:id, --where field:op:value, CSV --op in shorthands, each a pre-call transform in parseOpArgs), FGAP-064 (kebab-case flag normalization — a kebab->camel alias layer in parseOpArgs so conventional --dry-run / --id forms resolve to the camelCase op-schema keys), and FGAP-032 (the id-flag instance of the same root — --id aliases to the op's single declared id-param: --itemId/--parentId/--taskId/--unitId — resolved in the SAME normalization layer as FGAP-064). The ops still receive + require their declared params; the CLI normalizes/supplies them. Additive per the FEAT-008 invariant: every currently-working flag form keeps working, the op surface is byte-unchanged.
acceptance_criteria: To be determined exactly from live codebase.

Closes FGAP-027: pass whole:true into structureForRead at ops-registry.ts:1166 for the addressed-node case so the IN-PI read-schema op returns the full subtree (matching scripts/orchestrator/read-schema.ts:181), retaining the 50KB cap. tasks.description: Behavioral parity gate — the regression guard that locks in TASK-015/016/017/018. Asserts in-pi op <-> reflecting-CLI parity: the CLI is a faithful ADDITIVE reflection of the op-registry. The orchestrator scripts are composers/launch-support, NOT a parity reference; the two canonical routes are the in-pi op and the reflecting CLI. Extends scripts/parity-check.ts AFTER its existing hard static checks (op-coverage classification, ctx-forwarding, {json}-content-cap) with two new HARD op<->CLI checks, and RETIRES the soft op<->orchestrator-script comparison (checkDualSurfaceParity + its scriptsDir/DUAL_SURFACE machinery). Check 1 (required-but-derivable, FGAP-019 class): parse the CLI required-filter exemptions from cli.ts source and assert every config-derivable required op param (arrayKey, derived by injectArrayKey) is exempted by the CLI — FAILs if arrayKey is dropped from the filter or a new config-derivable required param is added without an exemption. Check 2 (output-shape, FGAP-020/027 class): run read-schema --path at an object node via BOTH routes in-process (the op run() and the CLI main() --json) on a fixture substrate and assert the op's r.read.data equals the CLI's output.data (boundedJsonOutput unwraps {read} so output === r.read) — FAILs on any op/CLI output divergence. In-process harness (not a subprocess of dist/bin.js) because husky pre-commit does not build dist. Folds into the same violations[]/exit-1 aggregation; main() becomes async. Converts the parity class from found-one-at-a-time-by-dogfooding into caught-at-commit. Closes no gap directly; durability mechanism.
acceptance_criteria: To be determined exactly from live codebase.
tasks.notes: Release-group: cli-parity (FEAT-008) | order 5/6 | regression guard (closes no gap) | depends on orders 1-4 (asserts their post-fix state) | REFRAMED from the 2026-06-05 op<->script premise (R-0003 cli-ops-scripts-parity-survey, now superseded) to op<->CLI: the orchestrator scripts are off the table as a parity reference

Empty/absent blocks behave as today; the schemas loop is UNCHANGED (schemas still honor --update). Render preserved in the install handler (index.ts:910-942). Add a regression test (none exists today). Per DEC-0010: also add a CHANGELOG [Unreleased] entry + usage-only docs (pkg README, monorepo README, SKILL via skill-narrative.md). Reuse readBlockForDir + forEachBlockArray (block-api.ts); installContext is the sole block-writer.

Add a bare-verb `pi-context update` (per cli-command-form) with a `--dry-run` verb flag, superseding `install --update` as the user-facing update path. Update consults checkStatus (index.ts:858) per installed schema and routes by drift state: in-sync / catalog-ahead -> the existing resync path; locally-modified / both-diverged -> refuse-and-report (do NOT overwrite) until T3 (TASK-036) upgrades that branch to merge. This slice delivers 'never silently clobber' immediately via refuse-and-report.

tasks.description: FEAT-006 T2 — base-stamping (the merge precondition). On every install/update, store the as-installed catalog schema BODY via the content-addressed object store: putObject(destRoot, asset.content_hash, schemaJson) at the baseline write (index.ts:738-754), keyed by the content_hash the asset already records (object-store.ts; content-hash.ts:82-84). Retrievable at merge time via getObject(destRoot, content_hash). This supplies the 3-way merge base that does not exist on disk today (checkStatus keeps only a SHA-256, context.ts:79). Depends on TASK-034.
acceptance_criteria: To be determined exactly from live codebase.

tasks.description: FEAT-006 T3 — deterministic schema-merge. Add a key/path-level draft-07 3-way merge mergeSchema(base, ours, theirs) -> {merged, conflicts[]}: base = the base-stamped as-installed body (getObject), ours = the installed dest schema, theirs = the catalog source schema. Wire it into the update flow at the locally-modified / both-diverged branch (replacing TASK-034's refuse-and-report): auto-apply the merged schema when the conflict set is empty; on a non-empty conflict set leave the schema unmodified and hand the typed set to the resolver (TASK-037). Depends on TASK-035; governed by DEC-0017.
acceptance_criteria: To be determined exactly from live codebase.

tasks.description: FEAT-006 T4 — conflict resolver. Route the typed conflict set from TASK-036 to a resolver: a pi-bound agent mergetool — dispatch a bounded pi-bound session granted read-schema + write-schema, feeding (base, ours, theirs); the agent proposes a reconciled schema and writes it via write-schema, forced through the existing dryRun + auth-gate (non-interactive refuses) — with graceful fallback to a rendered conflict report {path, base, ours, theirs} when no interactive/credentialed pi is available. The schema is left unmodified on the report path; the rest of the update proceeds either way. No change to auth-gate or pi-bound internals (grant + dispatch only). Depends on TASK-036; governed by DEC-0017.
acceptance_criteria: To be determined exactly from live codebase.

tasks.description: FEAT-006 T5 — config-registry propagation. On update, additively merge catalog-new config-registry entries (relation_types, invariants, block_kinds, lenses) that are ABSENT from the existing substrate config into it, preserving the user's existing/modified registries; surfaced in output and previewed by --dry-run. Independent of the schema-merge slices (disjoint seam: the installContext config spread, the FGAP-060 site). Closes FGAP-060.
acceptance_criteria: To be determined exactly from live codebase.

tasks.description: FEAT-006 T6 — surfaced mutation reporting + idempotent skip. The update output enumerates every mutation it performs (schema re-syncs/merges, migration-declaration registrations into migrations.json); --dry-run lists them without writing. And update skips rewriting an existing catalog-origin block file whose content already equals the starter. Independent of the schema-merge slices (disjoint seam: the install summary + the block branch). Closes FGAP-050 + FGAP-051.
acceptance_criteria: To be determined exactly from live codebase.

tasks.description: CLI discovery surface: a scannable grouped --help and a --version command, so a globally-installed reflecting CLI is legible and self-identifying. Both edit cli.ts's top-level dispatch (deriveTopHelp ~:407-423 + a --version short-circuit in main). Closes FGAP-062 (--help top-level listing is an unscannable dump of full op.descriptions with no grouping/sort and promptSnippet unused, and pi-bound is undiscoverable as a process-mode branch outside the registry — data-driven fix in deriveTopHelp from existing OpDefinition fields: source promptSnippet, group by name-prefix, add a process-modes section) and FGAP-063 (no --version/-v — add a --version short-circuit in main printing the package version). Per cli-command-form: --version is a top-level one-shot flag, pi-bound stays a bare verb. Additive per the FEAT-008 invariant: no op behavior changes; help/version are dispatch-layer reads only.
acceptance_criteria: To be determined exactly from live codebase.
tasks.notes: Release-group: cli-parity (FEAT-008) | order 6/6 | addresses FGAP-062, FGAP-063 | category: CLI dispatch/discovery | independent — no build-order dependency on the input/output tasks

tasks.description: Backfill decision derivation edges, then raise decision-shows-derivation to error. The decision-shows-derivation requires-edge invariant (the dedicated structural bite for the derive-decisions-from-facts convention) is live at severity WARNING: every decision must carry a decision_derived_from_item edge (the fact its resolution derives from) OR a decision_escalates_underdetermined edge (a genuinely-underdetermined escalated choice -> a framework-gap). 17 existing decisions currently warn. This task: (1) for each warning decision, read its reasoning and either edge decision_derived_from_item -> the substrate fact its resolution derived from (a research / convention / gap / prior-decision / rationale item; file a rationale item to reify a code/mandate fact that has no existing item), or decision_escalates_underdetermined -> the framework-gap capturing a genuinely-underdetermined choice; (2) once context-validate shows zero decision-shows-derivation warnings, raise the invariant severity from warning to error via amend-config. This completes the convention-articulation clean-after-backfill pattern for decision derivation, making a fork-as-decision (a decision with no shown derivation basis) UNFILEABLE.
acceptance_criteria: To be determined exactly from live codebase.
tasks.notes: governance enforcement | extends the FEAT-007 convention-articulation family to decision DERIVATION | the bite is already live at warning; this raises it to error after backfill

tasks.description: Per-op <op> --help best-of-breed template + machine-readable help — closes FGAP-072. Sibling to TASK-040 under FEAT-008 (different code location: deriveHelp + OpDefinition help-metadata + the help-render-through-format path, vs TASK-040's deriveTopHelp + main). Additive: in-pi op behavior frozen (run/parameters/description unchanged; the parity gate TASK-019 stays green — additive help-metadata is not op behavior). Scope, per FGAP-072's proposed_resolution: (1) add an examples[] help-metadata field (and optionally related[]) to OpDefinition in ops-registry.ts, and AUTHOR examples[] for ALL ~48 use-ops from the CLAUDE.md canonical filing patterns (no synthetic floor — the no-deferral/best-of-breed mandate requires real copy-pasteable examples per op); (2) render a fixed template in deriveHelp (cli.ts:554): SYNOPSIS (derived from the op's typed params in docopt notation — required -> `--flag <type>`, optional -> `[--flag <type>]`, @file alternation) -> summary -> Flags (existing required-vs-optional typed list) -> EXAMPLES -> RELATED -> learn-more footer; (3) derive RELATED from the op's name-prefix group (the same pure classifier TASK-040 builds for the top-level listing) and surface the process-mode pi-bound; (4) route a structured-help rendering ({synopsis, summary, flags[], examples[], related[]}) through the existing --format json (FGAP-021) so a per-op --help --format json is machine-parseable off the registry. Reuse TASK-040's name-prefix group classifier for RELATED so the two help surfaces share one grouping source (no drift; the parity gate guards it).
acceptance_criteria: To be determined exactly from live codebase.
tasks.notes: Release-group: cli-parity (FEAT-008) | order 7/7 | sibling to TASK-040 | addresses FGAP-072 | informed by R-0011 | folds the per-op help-template render layer into FEAT-008 so the CLI ships best-of-breed (the prior session left these elements parked; no-deferral requires folding, not deferring)

tasks.description: Add a reflected op `context-lens-view` keyed by --lensId that projects a config-declared lens as a binned item-view. Reuses loadLensView (packages/pi-context/src/lens-view.ts:43), renderLensView (lens-view.ts:105), and pageArray (read-element.ts:188). Serves every config.lenses[] entry — kind target, composition, and hand-curated. Modes: a bin SUMMARY (each declared bin with its item count) and, under --bin, that bin's items paged by --offset/--limit. Additive: a new op; all other in-pi ops byte-unchanged. Slotted into the cli.ts groupForOp classifier. Closes FGAP-073.
acceptance_criteria: To be determined exactly from live codebase.
tasks.notes: Closes FGAP-073 (all four target-lenses + composition/hand-curated). Reuses loadLensView / renderLensView / pageArray. Adjacent to FEAT-004 (config-driven state-derivation), distinct. docs-surface-sync: op description/promptSnippet/examples + README/SKILL audit + CHANGELOG [Unreleased].

tasks.description: Broaden the rhetorical-register checkpoint guard and clean the existing violating context atoms. Closes FGAP-074. Two parts: (1) extend the register-checkpoint hook (.claude/hooks/, currently framework-gaps-only) to cover append AND update/closure writes for tasks, verification, decisions, issues, research, features, and framework-gaps; exclude session-notes. (2) Rewrite every existing violating block body (the ~118 items in the FGAP-074 audit) to declarative current-truth per rhetorical-register, preserving all load-bearing content.
acceptance_criteria: To be determined exactly from live codebase.
tasks.notes: Closes FGAP-074. The guard is a review-checkpoint forcing-function (@file-agnostic); broadening changes only the block/op match-set + adds update/closure coverage. Cleaning is a per-item declarative rewrite preserving substance; session-notes excluded (rule 6). Audit baseline: analysis/2026-06-09-rhetorical-register-block-audit.md.

tasks.description: Fix the check-changelog watched-surface over-flag — closes FGAP-075. Add a subtractive isExemptSurface(path) predicate to scripts/check-changelog.ts and filter changedPaths through it at the top of changedPackages, so build-excluded *.test.ts and the monitor packages/pi-behavior-monitors/examples/*.patterns.json learned-pattern stores no longer count as published surface. Orthogonal to watchDirsFromFiles/pathInWatchSet/unreleasedGrew; a genuine src/*.ts or examples/*.monitor.json still flags.
acceptance_criteria: To be determined exactly from live codebase.
tasks.notes: Closes FGAP-075. Subtractive predicate + filter only; no change to the watch-set derivation or matching. The gate fix lives in scripts/ (not a watched package surface), so committing it does not self-trip. Unblocks the TASK-043 composition-test + hedge.patterns.json commit. Separate, noted-not-fixed: a monitor test mutates the shipped hedge.patterns.json on each run (test-hygiene).

tasks.description: Make pi-context update --dryRun predict the precise catalog-ahead outcome — closes FGAP-066. In updateContext's catalog-ahead dry branch (packages/pi-context/src/index.ts:1239-1262), replace the unconditional `resynced` push with a READ-ONLY simulation of the live resync: find the catalog migration chain, forward-migrate the populated block in memory, and AJV-validate the migrated items against the new schema, classifying the schema as resynced / migrated / blocked exactly as the live resyncSchema (:599-641) would — without writing. Report migrationsRegistered only for non-blocked outcomes (a live blocked run rolls the decls back). Dry and live outcomes must be identical for every catalog-ahead schema.
acceptance_criteria: To be determined exactly from live codebase.
tasks.notes: Closes FGAP-066. Scope is the dry-branch faithfulness only — the live path (resyncSchema) is already correct. Empirical grounding + reproduction: R-0012 / analysis/2026-06-10-update-dryrun-live-and-transactional-seams.md. The registry-propagation-on-blocked transactional question is a SEPARATE finding (a decision + a legibility gap), not this task.

tasks.description: Add the advancer-completion invariant class to the validator — closes FGAP-082. Extend InvariantDecl (context.ts:202-214) and validateContext's invariant executor with a third config-declarable class: for a source item entering when_bucket (complete), every target reached via the declared relation_types (task_advances_story / feature_advances_story) must be met — met = at least one incoming advancer edge whose endpoint item is in met_bucket (complete) — with self_satisfies so the completing item counts as its own stories' advancer (the single-advancer case must not deadlock). Severity error at context-validate. Code change: full canonical pipeline.
acceptance_criteria: To be determined exactly from live codebase.
tasks.notes: Closes FGAP-082. Enables enforcement of the user-stories completion rule (analysis/2026-06-10-user-stories-block-DRAFT.md); the rule's config declaration lands with this task. Engine grounding: analysis/2026-06-10-story-completion-invariant-engine-audit.md. Write-time blocking (a completeTask/write-path hook) is out of scope — context-validate error only.

tasks.description: Surface the per-item validation diagnostic on update's blocked path + as a standalone op — closes FGAP-077. Bind the ValidationError resyncSchema's unbound catch discards (index.ts:622): map each AJV error's instancePath (/<arrayKey>/N) to the item id, thread {item id, field, keyword, params, value} per failing item into the update result's blocked entry (structured + CLI text), distinguish the no-chain block from the validation block, and expose the same returns-not-throws per-item validator as a standalone op (validate a block's items against a target schema version -> per-item failures). Builds on TASK-046's in-memory migrate+validate helper.
acceptance_criteria: To be determined exactly from live codebase.
tasks.notes: Closes FGAP-077; advances STORY-001/002/003/004/005/009. Depends on TASK-046 (the shared in-memory migrate+validate primitive). Grounding: analysis/2026-06-10-update-blocked-diagnostic-task-DRAFT.md user stories + R-0012.

tasks.description: Expose check-status as a reflected CLI op and report the version gap — closes FGAP-030 + FGAP-078. Add the context-check-status op (run -> checkStatus(cwd), the existing CheckStatusReport) so the drift report is reachable from the reflecting CLI, and extend the report/render to state, per behind schema, the baseline->catalog version delta — covering content-only drift (catalog-ahead is a hash comparison; a schema can be behind with no version-number change). One surface: the checkStatus report + its render + the op entry.
acceptance_criteria: To be determined exactly from live codebase.
tasks.notes: Closes FGAP-030 + FGAP-078 (same surface: the drift report + its exposure); advances STORY-007. Independent of the other slices.

tasks.description: Make the catalog schema locally diffable — closes FGAP-079. Provide the catalog schema BODY to the operator without hunting node_modules: a single read-only reflected op that fetches and prints the catalog schema verbatim (resolveCatalog samplesRoot + kind.schema_path; read-samples-catalog returns only a projection today). No sidecar.
acceptance_criteria: To be determined exactly from live codebase.
tasks.notes: Closes FGAP-079; advances STORY-010. Independent of the other slices. Grounding: analysis/2026-06-10-block-name-vs-kind-id-resolution-gap.md is unrelated; the catalog-access grounding is the user-stories DRAFT + the explore that located resolveCatalog/samplesRoot.

tasks.description: Persist a pending-blocked state and add the resolve-blocked / update --continue commit op — closes FGAP-080. When resyncSchema blocks, persist what the rollback currently discards (target schema body/version, the migration chain, the per-item failure set from TASK-048's diagnostic), and add a commit op mirroring resolve-conflict: re-validate the corrected block against the target schema, on success write the new schema via writeSchemaCheckedForDir and advance the merge base via stampBaselineFromBody — so the schema converges and stops re-blocking. The diagnose -> fix -> retry loop, end-to-end, matching the conflicts/resolve-conflict model.
acceptance_criteria: To be determined exactly from live codebase.
tasks.notes: Closes FGAP-080; advances STORY-006/011/012/014. Depends on TASK-048 (the persisted failure set is its diagnostic). Reuses stampBaselineFromBody, writeSchemaCheckedForDir, validateBlockWithMigrationForDir, the object store.

tasks.description: Write in-file failure markers into a blocked block — closes FGAP-081. A blocked schema's failures appear at the point of fix: git-merge-style markers written INTO the block file at the offending items/fields (the per-item failure set from TASK-048), the pre-marker block recoverable from the object store. Markers are the DEFAULT behavior of a live validation-failed block (STORY-013: the operator expects them; no flag, no mode — the supersession of the byte-unchanged contract for the block file is user-decided); a dry run never writes, and a no-chain block has no failures to mark. Markers are stripped by the TASK-051 resolution commit (resolve-blocked), which refuses while the named violations persist. Implementation resolves the marker shape against identity-stamping (content_hash recompute) and pre-write validation — the two constraints writeBlockForDir imposes.
acceptance_criteria: To be determined exactly from live codebase.
tasks.notes: Closes FGAP-081; advances STORY-013/014. Depends on TASK-051 (the resolution loop the markers feed). The contract supersession (in-file markers, object-store recoverability) is user-decided; pairs with DEC-0018.

tasks.description: Behavior-preserving refinement of the update/marker machinery. (1) Extract the triplicated in-memory migration-registry seeding into one helper in migration-registry-loader.ts — buildFreshRegistryWithChain(substrateDir, chain): substrate on-disk decls + the extra chain decls, deduped on (schemaName, fromVersion) with the substrate decl winning, cache-untouched — and swap the three call sites (simulateResyncOutcome, validateBlockItemsAgainstCatalog, resolveBlocked). The on-disk appendMigrationDeclForDir loops and the live path's cache-warming getProjectMigrationRegistryForDir are a different operation and stay untouched. (2) Hoist the marker sentinel-line pattern (/^(<{7}|>{7})/, inlined at four detection/strip sites) into one named module constant kept in lockstep with the tokens composeMarkerText emits.
acceptance_criteria: To be determined exactly from live codebase.
tasks.notes: Refinement debt surfaced by the post-TASK-052 code-simplifier evaluation and confirmed by investigation: the three seeding blocks are behaviorally identical (same dedup, same substrate-wins precedence, same error posture; the loop-vs-spread spelling is cosmetic); the sentinel regex is the invariant most likely to break silently under future edits. Internal-only — no exported-surface, op, or schema change.

tasks.description: Behavior-preserving refinement, the code-simplifier evaluation's finding #2 verbatim: Duplicated raw tmp+rename marker-write block (2 sites) — packages/pi-context/src/index.ts:2026-2028 (mark) and :2426-2428 (unmark). Identical three-line shape: const tmpPath = `${blockFile}.<verb>-${process.pid}.tmp`; fs.writeFileSync(tmpPath, text); fs.renameSync(tmpPath, blockFile);. A small local helper rawWriteBlockText(blockFile, text, verb) would DRY this and name why it bypasses writeTypedFile (the marker text / stripped text is written outside the AJV-validating writer deliberately). The writeTypedFile atomic writer at block-api.ts:870 cannot be reused (it validates), so a dedicated raw helper is the right level. Behavior-preserving: same tmp path scheme, same atomic rename. (Line numbers are as evaluated pre-TASK-053; relocate by content.)
acceptance_criteria: To be determined exactly from live codebase.
tasks.notes: Surfaced by the post-TASK-052 code-simplifier evaluation (its optional finding #2); filed verbatim on user direction. Internal-only — no exported-surface, op, or schema change.

tasks.description: Add a pre-flight committed-substrate check to update — warn or refuse on a dirty substrate — with guidance text naming `git tag` as the restore-point workflow, visible in three places: (1) the runtime dirty-substrate message printed by update says "commit or git tag first to keep a restore point" — the operator sees it at exactly the moment it matters; (2) the update op's description/promptSnippet in ops-registry, which is also what reflection and SKILL.md render; (3) SKILL.md + READMEs via skill-narrative.md regen and the package/root README update sections, per the normal docs-surface-sync pass. No new doc artifact — the text rides the op's existing strings and the new warning. No substrate-tag op: per DEC-0004 (enacted) git owns refs/snapshots; the substrate including objects/ is git-tracked, so `git tag` before update + `git checkout <tag> -- <substrate-dir>` is the restore workflow.
acceptance_criteria: To be determined exactly from live codebase.
tasks.notes: User-granted G1-derived filing (the operator-story think-out): the safety gap (update mutates with no committed-baseline pre-flight) + the knowledge gap (no surface names the git-tag restore workflow) closed in one item. Prior-art sweep found no existing item covering snapshot/restore or an update pre-flight.

tasks.description: Enrich resolve-blocked's success report: running resolve-blocked exactly as today prints a summary that reads the way `git show --stat` reads after a merge — what was written, what changed, from-state → to-state. The command surface itself is untouched: no new op, no new flag. The report carries the old vs new schema version, which items were forward-migrated (or left unchanged), and the merge-base advance from→to — facts already computed in memory at the success point and currently discarded (today's output is effectively `resolved: true` plus one hash, and `registeredMigrations` is empty in the normal case because the blocking update run already registered the chain).
acceptance_criteria: To be determined exactly from live codebase.
tasks.notes: Origin: the operator-story think-out — the D2 and G5 statements (confirmed duplicates; one filing). D2's proposed --diff flag spelling is not adopted: the command surface is untouched. Same legibility principle as FGAP-076 (update's partial-application report).

tasks.description: Schema version-history audit (the `git log <file>` analog): a read-only command (e.g. `read-schema-history --schemaName <name>`) that prints the schema's history — which migrations ran, when, and from what base — plus the two recording fixes that make the history complete: stamp an applied-at time when a migration runs, and keep a pointer from each new baseline to the one it replaced. Without those two, the command could only show a partial story and "when" would still require digging through git commits: today the only timestamp on a migration decl is its authoring time, and each baseline advance overwrites the prior {content_hash, version} with no parent pointer (the replaced bodies sit unreferenced in the object store). The command composes what the substrate records: the registered migration decls for the schema (from→to, kind, author, authored-at, applied-at), the baseline lineage, and the installed/catalog versions.
acceptance_criteria: To be determined exactly from live codebase.
tasks.notes: Origin: the operator-story think-out — the G2 statement ("As an operator, after a migration has been applied, I want to see the version history of a schema — which migrations ran, when, and from what base — so I can audit how the schema reached its current state. (git log <file> analog.)"). Scope corrected per the status-quo challenge: applied-at + baseline lineage are in-scope recording fixes, not flagged out — they are event metadata, not git refs/DAG machinery, so DEC-0004 does not speak to them.

tasks.description: Walk the migration chain incrementally (the `git bisect` analog): a read-only command (e.g. `walk-migration-chain --schemaName <name>`) that applies the schema's migration chain one step at a time in memory — apply migration 1, validate, apply migration 2, validate — to isolate which step breaks. Per step it reports the step's decl (from→to, kind) and which items' content changed; validation runs against the target schema on each cumulative prefix, attributing the first failure to the step whose application introduced it. Nothing is written — the walk is entirely in memory over the live block data and the resolved chain (the catalog chain, or the pinned pending-blocked chain when the schema is blocked). Limitation stated honestly: the catalog ships only the latest schema body per kind, so a step's output cannot be validated against an intermediate schema version (only against the final target, or an intermediate body that happens to be pinned in the object store); the op isolates the breaking transform step, which is the operator's ask.
acceptance_criteria: To be determined exactly from live codebase.
tasks.notes: Origin: the operator-story think-out — the G4 statement ("As an operator, when I suspect a specific migration in the chain introduced a validation failure, I want to walk the chain incrementally — apply migration 1, validate, apply migration 2, validate — to isolate which step breaks. (git bisect analog.)"). Reuses the existing chain machinery (fresh in-memory registry, step-ordered application, per-item failure mapping) — no new parallel machinery.

tasks.description: Reflect the install ceremony as a context-install OpDefinition in ops-registry.ts, surfacing the existing installContext engine (index.ts:1211) on the CLI like its five lifecycle siblings (context-init / context-accept-all / context-switch / context-list / context-archive). No engine fork — the op calls installContext; writer-classified via the DEC-0006 ctx channel that context-init/context-accept-all already carry; remove or update its INTENTIONALLY_UNEXPOSED_WRITERS entry (ops-registry.ts:2171-2175). Makes the init -> accept-all -> install bootstrap fully scriptable from the CLI with no pi session. Closes FGAP-088.
acceptance_criteria: To be determined exactly from live codebase.
tasks.notes: User-directed fix-task for FGAP-088 (the install ceremony has no reflected CLI op). One of the two tasks that make the TASK-033 runtime demo completable as plain CLI. Grounding: analysis/2026-06-13-install-surface-and-guard-scope-gaps.md; FGAP-030 is the resolution precedent (a command-only surface unreachable from the CLI resolved by adding the reflected op).

tasks.description: Scope the PreToolUse enforcement hooks to the active substrate. Make .claude/hooks/gap-register-guard.sh and .claude/hooks/block-pi-context-glue.sh fire only when the write/invocation targets the active substrate — parse --cwd from the command string (default to the project cwd when absent), resolve .pi-context.json contextDir at that cwd, and guard only active-substrate targets; a write/invocation whose --cwd names a non-active/throwaway substrate passes. The not-installed block-substrate-cli-bypass.sh spec (analysis/2026-06-06-substrate-cli-bypass-prevention-hook-spec.md) is the resource-scoped recognizer pattern. Closes FGAP-089.
acceptance_criteria: To be determined exactly from live codebase.
tasks.notes: User-directed fix-task for FGAP-089 (enforcement hooks scoped by op-shape, not target-substrate). One of the two tasks that make the TASK-033 runtime demo completable as plain CLI. The hooks are local gitignored files; the criteria are testable by piping a synthetic tool_input JSON to each hook and checking the exit code. Grounding: analysis/2026-06-13-install-surface-and-guard-scope-gaps.md.

tasks.description: Add three relation_types to the pi-context registry at BOTH loci (active .context config via amend-config --registry relation_types --operation add; and the packaged catalog packages/pi-context/samples/conception.json relation_types[] — the durable home, since mergeCatalogRegistries is additive catalog->config and a config-only add would not survive a fresh accept-all): decision_raises_gap (category data_flow, source_kinds [decisions], target_kinds [framework-gaps]); decision_gated_by_item (category ordering, source_kinds [decisions], target_kinds [*]); gap_relates_to_gap (category data_flow, source_kinds [framework-gaps], target_kinds [framework-gaps]). Pure config/catalog addition, no .ts or schema change (the TASK-033 template: item_governed_by_convention was catalog-only). Resolves the expressibility half of FGAP-090. category is inert in code today (derivation selects edges by literal relation_type name, never reads category), so decision_gated_by_item:ordering is forward-looking vocabulary a future FGAP-061 deriver consumes by name; added now, valid, inert until that deriver lands.
acceptance_criteria: To be determined exactly from live codebase.

tasks.description: Add a write-time edge kind/category validator. When an edge is written via appendRelationByRef (packages/pi-context/src/context-sdk.ts:1412) and appendRelationsByRef (:1549), check the parent item's kind against the relation_type's source_kinds and the child's against target_kinds (with the * wildcard) and the edge against the relation_type's category, and REJECT a mismatched edge at write with a clear error naming the offending endpoint kind and relation_type. This hoists the existing post-hoc kind check (validateContext, context-sdk.ts:1987-2021, FGAP-086/DEC-0037) to the write surface (the raw appendRelationsForDir, context.ts:649, cannot resolve endpoint kinds under the documented layering at context.ts:617-625). Presence-gate on source_kinds/target_kinds exactly as the validate-time check (context-sdk.ts:1990) so currently-passing writes are unaffected. Resolves the write-time-enforcement half of FGAP-090. Distinct from TASK-027 (ordering-edge endpoint-role DIRECTION, ordering-only; this checks source/target KIND + category across all relation_types).
acceptance_criteria: To be determined exactly from live codebase.

tasks.description: Apply FGAP-090's new vocabulary to correct the mistyped/absent edges (depends on TASK-A). In order: (1) File DEC-0018's true forcing artifact as a framework-gaps item — the problem that forced DEC-0018: pi-context update's post-loop registry propagation writes config.json even when a schema returns blocked, ungated on the schema-loop outcome (packages/pi-context/src/index.ts:1391-1421); this is the cause DEC-0018 ruled on (affirming per-component), distinct from FGAP-076 which is the legibility consequence DEC-0018 spawned. (2) Add a decision_addresses_gap edge DEC-0018 -> the new forcing gap, restoring genuine decision-cites-forcing-artifact satisfaction (cause, not consequence). (3) Retype the DEC-0018 -> FGAP-076 edge from decision_addresses_gap to decision_raises_gap via replace-relation — non-breaking once step 2 is in place. (4) Add a decision_gated_by_item edge DEC-0018 -> FGAP-076 (gated thing = parent, gate = child). (5) Add gap_relates_to_gap edges FGAP-090 -> FGAP-007 and FGAP-090 -> FGAP-061. Resolves the edge-correction half of FGAP-090 and repairs DEC-0018's backwards forcing-artifact citation. The step-1 forcing-artifact filing is its own provenance-stopped write.
acceptance_criteria: To be determined exactly from live codebase.

tasks.description: Backfill the warranted-but-absent closure-table edges that early tasks' filed text entails but that were never written — the concrete instances of FGAP-091's under-filing. The relation-graph completeness audit (analysis/2026-06-14-substrate-relation-graph-completeness-audit.md) enumerated: TASK-010 task_governed_by_decision -> DEC-0008 and task_addresses_gap -> FGAP-012; TASK-012 task_governed_by_decision -> DEC-0009 and task_addresses_gap -> FGAP-015; TASK-014 task_addresses_gap -> FGAP-014. First re-confirm the complete warranted-but-absent set via find-references against the items' filed text (including any symmetric sibling such as TASK-011), then write each absent edge via append-relation — each kind-correct, accepted by the write-time validator. Pure data backfill, no code. Partial addressing of FGAP-091 (the concrete missing edges; the forcing-function invariant is FGAP-091's separate residual).
acceptance_criteria: To be determined exactly from live codebase.

tasks.description: Extend `currentState` (packages/pi-context/src/context-sdk.ts:682-825) — the derivation behind context-current-state / `/context status` — so task readiness honors `task_gated_by_item` gates, not only `task_depends_on_task`. A planned task carrying a `task_gated_by_item` edge to a target NOT in the `complete` bucket must be reported blocked (target id in `blockedBy`) and excluded from `nextActions`; a gate target reaching the `complete` bucket releases the gate. Gate satisfaction reuses `bucket(target) === "complete"` via resolveStatusVocabulary (status-vocab.ts) — kind-general, the same mechanism the status-consistency invariant engine uses (context-sdk.ts:2186-2227); NOT a net-new completeness check. Both consumption sites fold gate parents: `blocked` (incompleteDeps→depParentsOf, :727-730/:742-751) and the `nextActions` topo predecessor function (:790). Scope is strictly the literal relation_type `task_gated_by_item`; `decision_gated_by_item` and non-task `*_gated_by_item` are inert (currentState buckets only tasks) and OUT of scope — the config-driven generalization is FEAT-004. NOW slice of FGAP-061.
acceptance_criteria: To be determined exactly from live codebase.

tasks.description: Back-port the relation_types consumed by shipped pi-context derivations but missing from the packaged catalog packages/pi-context/samples/conception.json relation_types[] (the single source of truth): `task_gated_by_item` (already in live .context/config.json — catalog-only add) and `phase_depends_on` (in NEITHER catalog nor live config — add to BOTH; the config add via amend-config). Before adding `phase_depends_on`, confirm roadmap-plan.ts (which consumes it at :329/:505/:528) is shipped/reachable, not dead code — if dead, surface that as the finding instead of registering the relation. Update the samples-catalog.test.ts relation_type count pin (mirror TASK-061's 31→34 bump). Entry shape per existing catalog entries: {canonical_id, display_name, category, source_kinds, target_kinds}. NOW back-port slice of FGAP-094; mirrors TASK-061/TASK-033 (ship vocabulary in the catalog).
acceptance_criteria: To be determined exactly from live codebase.

tasks.description: Build-time forcing-function preventing recurrence of FGAP-094's catalog↔config vocabulary drift: a gate that mechanically enumerates the relation_types consumed by literal string in shipped packages/pi-context/src/** derivations (the AST-reading pattern scripts/parity-check.ts already uses) and asserts the catalog packages/pi-context/samples/conception.json relation_types[] ⊇ that consumed set; a non-empty difference exits non-zero naming the missing relation_types. Wire into .husky/pre-commit AND .github/workflows/ci.yml sequenced alongside the existing parity-check.ts / check-changelog.ts gates, with a unit test mirroring parity-check.test.ts. At that task's plan step, derive whether to extend parity-check.ts with an added check-category or add a sibling scripts/catalog-vocab-parity.ts (parity-check.ts is an extensible gate aggregator). Include the explicit record classifying each live-only relation_type substrate-local-vs-catalog-worthy with its derivation (endpoint blocks shipped? consumed by a shipped derivation?). FORWARD class-fix slice of FGAP-094; mirrors FGAP-010's scripts/parity-check.ts structural answer for the catalog↔config vocabulary surface. Depends on TASK-066 (the gate goes green once the back-port lands).
acceptance_criteria: To be determined exactly from live codebase.

tasks.description: Make issues a gap-sibling first-class open-work kind: a task addresses an issue as it addresses a framework-gap; an open issue ranks and focuses in currentState as an open gap does; a completed task addressing an unresolved issue is invariant-flagged. Add the parity vocabulary in live .context config and the packaged catalog, plus two additive issue-schema fields. The deriver and lens-view are kind-general — change no deriver code. Preserve the divergences in criterion 7. Prerequisite for addressing issue-004 by a task.
acceptance_criteria: To be determined exactly from live codebase.
tasks.notes: Addresses FGAP-098. Prerequisite for addressing issue-004 by a task. Catalog back-port interacts with FGAP-094, TASK-066, TASK-067.

tasks.description: Make the operator pi-context binary a publish-free local copy of the working tree, immune to dev builds. Today /opt/homebrew/bin/pi-context is an npm link symlink into packages/pi-context-cli/dist/, so npm run build (rm -rf dist && tsc) repoints and transiently removes the live operator binary. Retire the link; install the operator binary as a packed copy of the working-tree CLI + its @davidorex dependency set (current code, not the stale registry 0.31.0); add a promote:cli root npm-task fronting scripts/promote-cli.mjs (build, pack the set, install into the global prefix, verify a non-symlink copy), fitting the version:* / release:* composer convention. Scope: the binary coupling only — not the proving-ground harness, a CI gate, or schema-evolution work.
acceptance_criteria: To be determined exactly from live codebase.
tasks.notes: Addresses issue-004. Scope: the binary coupling only.

tasks.description: Add a load-time config-migration path to loadConfigForDir (context.ts:564-581): read the config's schema_version and, on mismatch with the bundled config schema version, walk the schema-name-keyed migration chain for config before validating — mirroring validateBlockWithMigrationForDir (schema-validator.ts:205-242). Add a config migration registry entry and the declarative-transform primitive (map_each) that maps array elements within a migration step.
acceptance_criteria: To be determined exactly from live codebase.
tasks.notes: Design: analysis/2026-06-18-config-migration-corrected-implementation-steps.md (+ -design-feasibility, -proposal-validation).

tasks.description: Add a sanctioned config-repair path that applies the corrective mutation and validates the result, rather than loading-and-validating the stored config before the repair, so an already-invalid config is recoverable. Scope: any invalid config regardless of version, distinct from the version-gap migration. The repair mechanism is a `force`/`repair` opt on amend-config that bypasses the pre-load validation and validates the result via the existing write path (block-api.ts:892); derived from the existing op surface, no DEC.
acceptance_criteria: To be determined exactly from live codebase.
tasks.notes: Repair mechanism is DERIVED from the existing op surface, not deferred to a DECISION. Derivation: amend-config already mutates config and validates the RESULT before write (block-api.ts:892-895); the only thing blocking repair is its pre-load validation (context.ts:1251); and amend-config already carries an opt (dryRun, context.ts:1229). So config-repair is a validation-skip mode of amend-config — an opt on the existing op, not a separate recover op (which would duplicate amend-config's mutate+validate flow for a capability that is not missing). No DEC and no task_governed_by_decision edge; the choice is determined by the existing amend-config surface (derive-decisions 1b — an existing surface/convention).

tasks.description: Add a build-time gate that flags a non-additive config.schema.json diff — a removed or renamed key, or a new required field under the root additionalProperties:false — unless paired with a registered migration. Adopt the expand-contract discipline: new fields land optional; existing fields are not removed or renamed until a later contract release.
acceptance_criteria: To be determined exactly from live codebase.
tasks.notes: Build-time-gate family with FGAP-094 (TASK-067).

tasks.description: Stamp schema_version onto block data on the write path (block-api.ts:870-938, 1758-1767) and admit a single optional top-level schema_version on block schema envelopes, so the read-time AJV hook (block-api.ts:785-789) and the version-mismatch migration branch (schema-validator.ts:225-239) become active for all blocks. The stamp location is the block envelope (top-level schema_version); per-item stamping is out of scope (block-api.ts:782-784).
acceptance_criteria: To be determined exactly from live codebase.
tasks.notes: Stamp location is the block envelope (top-level schema_version), derived: the read/write hooks already key on envelope.schema_version (block-api.ts:785-789, 1758-1767). Not a deferred DECISION.

tasks.description: Add a sanctioned op that materializes any declared-but-absent block data file — the missing.blocks[] set from findUnmaterializedAssets — into an existing substrate, copying the catalog starter via the install path. Invoked by update for a catalog-added block kind and available standalone as a repair/materialize op; previewed by --dryRun.
acceptance_criteria: To be determined exactly from live codebase.
tasks.notes: Unblocks milestone activation.
